adds.rationale: Global X Robotics & AI ETF fills the profile's only unrepresented technology next-wave (robotics) as the 2023 AI surge accelerates demand for industrial automation; BOTZ has traded since September 2016 and its January 2023 prospectus confirms four robotics-plus-AI sub-themes — a clean, liquid on-ramp.

news_evidence.summary: Global X 497K (Jan 31, 2023) identifies four BOTZ themes: Industrial Robotics & Automation, Drones, AI, and Non-Industrial Robotics; >=80% in the Indxx Global Robotics & AI index.

Defense One (Sep 12, 2023): European defense spending growth is 'sluggish' despite Ukraine, bottlenecked by production — defense ETFs lack a near-term catalyst, reinforcing robotics over defense as the gap to fill.

removes.rationale: Apple is the weakest AI-thesis holder: China's September 2023 government iPhone ban — expanding from central agencies to state-owned enterprises — threatens ~one-fifth of revenue, while Huawei's Mate 60 5G launch adds structural competition in the same market, making AAPL the poorest near-term risk/reward of the four AI mega-caps.

news_evidence.summary: Apple fell 2.8% then 3.6% after China barred iPhones from central government agencies and signaled extension to state-backed entities; Huawei launched a made-in-China 5G phone at the same time.
Apple poised to shed $194B in market value as China broadens the iPhone ban; Huawei Mate 60 adds competitive pressure in Apple's second-largest market.

rationale_overall: One swap: AAPL exits, BOTZ enters, keeping the watchlist at 8. Apple's China iPhone ban — a concrete dated headwind that wiped ~$200B of market cap in two days — makes it the weakest risk/reward among the four AI mega-caps; MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA retain the cleaner AI thesis. BOTZ gives the portfolio its first robotics-wave position. The macro backdrop reinforces conservatism (10-yr Treasury 4.57%, S&P 500 -4.77% in September), biasing toward highest-conviction names. URA, RKLB, QTUM, SPY survive on thesis support (uranium term pricing >$60/lb on utility re-contracting; RKLB growing bookings + a May 2023 NASA contract; QTUM the quantum anchor). Post-change: AI (MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA), robotics (BOTZ), rockets (RKLB), quantum (QTUM), nuclear (URA), general markets (SPY).
